you_re_not_doing_it_on_purpose: You Re Not Doing It On Purpose
and_you_are_definitely_not_alo: And You Are Definitely Not Alo
it_shows_up_differently_for_ev: It Shows Up Differently For Ev
your_brain_has_learned_that_pu: Your Brain Has Learned That Pu
its_more_than_just_a_habit__its_a_powerf: It's more than just a habit — it's a powerful urge that can feel impossible to resist.
trichotillomania_is_a_condition_where_a_: Trichotillomania is a condition where a person feels powerful, often irresistible urges to pull out their own hair — from the scalp, eyebrows, or eyelashes. While it may provide a temporary sense of relief or satisfaction, it often leads to deep feelings of shame and social isolation. Many spend years feeling alone without ever knowing help exists.
understanding_your_experience_is_the_fir: Understanding your experience is the first step toward self-compassion. Recovery is a journey, and you've just begun a vital part of it.
a_building_tension_or_restlessness_just_: A building tension or restlessness just before pulling
a_brief_sense_of_relief_or_release_when_: A brief sense of relief or release when pulling happens
guilt_shame_or_distress_shortly_after: Guilt, shame or distress shortly after
hair_pulling_from_the_scalp_eyebrows_or_: Hair pulling from the scalp, eyebrows or eyelashes
nail_biting_or_picking_at_the_skin: Nail biting or picking at the skin
sometimes_automatic_while_watching_tv_or: Sometimes automatic (while watching TV or reading)
powerful_often_irresistible_urges_to_pul: Powerful, often irresistible urges to pull hair from the scalp, eyebrows, or other areas.
a_sense_of_mounting_tension_before_pulli: A sense of mounting tension before pulling, or when attempting to resist.
a_feeling_of_relief_satisfaction_or_grat: A feeling of relief, satisfaction, or gratification after pulling.
frequent_attempts_to_stop_or_cut_back_on: Frequent attempts to stop or cut back on hair pulling.
significant_distress_or_impairment_in_so: Significant distress or impairment in social, work, or other areas of life.
hrt_desc: Break the cycle of hair pulling by training your brain to choose a competing response.
awareness_desc: Identify the moments when the urge to pull is strongest. Notice the physical sensations — an itch, tension, or restlessness.
competing_desc: When the urge strikes, immediately engage in a physical action that makes pulling impossible, like clenching your fists or sitting on your hands.
hrt_completion_desc: You've completed this introductory session. The key to habit reversal is consistent practice during real-world urges.
sensory_intro: Replace the sensation of pulling with a different, safe sensory experience.
sensory_options: Try different textures: a soft velvet cloth, a prickly fidget ball, or a smooth stone. The goal is to satisfy the sensory urge without damage.
sensory_completion_desc: Keep exploring different textures until you find the one that works best for you. Carry your sensory tool with you at all times.
